title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: Les sons du français
days.objective: Learn the French sounds that spelling hides, so you can read and hear French correctly.
days.paretoFocus: French isn't phonetic like it looks: master the nasal vowels, silent endings and a few accents once, and pronunciation stops being a mystery.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 sound/pronunciation activity.
Repeat the nasal vowels on/an/in aloud.
Read 10 words aloud from the cheatsheet.

items: Say the three nasal vowels aloud.
Recall: is a final consonant usually pronounced?
Read 'bonjour, ça va ?' with correct sounds.

activities.title: Les sons clés
activities.intro: French letters combine into sounds. The big three to master are the nasal vowels (air through the nose) and the silent final consonants. Say each aloud.

items.en: final consonants are usually silent: peti(t), vou(s)

questions.prompt: Which word contains a NASAL vowel?
questions.explanation: 'ain' in pain is a nasal vowel; the others are oral.
Most final consonants in French are silent.

questions.prompt: Add the missing accent: m__re (mother). Type the accented letter only.
Build the French for: 'Hello, how's it going?'

activities.title: Se saluer
activities.intro: A first tiny exchange. Read both parts aloud, then just the 'Me' lines.

days.title: Bonjour et politesse
days.objective: Greet, thank and say goodbye politely in both registers.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of fixed politeness formulas cover most first contacts. Learn the tu/vous difference now.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'greetings' activity.
Practise tu vs vous with 5 example people.
Review today's words in the Words tab.

items: Recall bonjour / bonsoir / salut and when to use each.
Say 's'il vous plaît' and 's'il te plaît'.

activities.title: Formules de politesse
activities.intro: French politeness is a fixed set of phrases. 'vous' is formal/plural; 'tu' is informal.

questions.prompt: You greet your boss in the morning:
questions.explanation: Bonjour is the standard, polite daytime greeting.

questions.prompt: 's'il vous plaît' is the ___ form of 'please'.
questions.explanation: Use s'il te plaît with people you address as 'tu'.

questions.prompt: How do you thank someone strongly?
Build the French for: 'A coffee, please.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour. Un café, s'il vous plaît.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Here you are. Will that be all?

days.title: Se présenter
days.objective: Introduce yourself: name, nationality and where you're from.
days.paretoFocus: Three patterns — Je m'appelle…, Je suis…, Je viens de… — carry every first introduction.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'se présenter' activity.
Say your own full introduction aloud 5 times.
Learn 10 new words in the Words tab.

items: Give your name, nationality and city from memory.
Recall Je m'appelle / Je suis / Je viens de.

activities.title: Se présenter
activities.intro: The core self-introduction phrases. Nationalities change for men/women.

items.en: My name is Marie. (lit. I call myself)

questions.prompt: A woman says 'I am a student':
Complete: J'___ à Lyon. (I live in Lyon)
Build the French for: 'I come from Belgium.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: My name is Alex. And you?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, c'est Camille. Vous venez d'où ?
lines.en: I'm Camille. Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je viens des États-Unis. J'habite à Zagreb.
lines.en: I'm from the United States. I live in Zagreb.

days.title: Les nombres et l'âge
days.objective: Count and give your age and phone number.
days.paretoFocus: Numbers 0-20 plus the tens unlock prices, times, ages and phone numbers — daily survival.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'numbers' activity.
Count aloud 0-20, then the tens to 60.
Say your age and phone number in French.

items: Say your age in a full sentence.

activities.title: Les nombres
activities.intro: Count aloud. Note the tricky teens and the tens.

items.en: I am thirty. (lit. I have thirty years)

questions.prompt: To say your age you use the verb…
questions.explanation: Age uses avoir: j'ai 30 ans, NOT je suis.
an = year; always plural after a number > 1: ans.

days.title: Le genre et les articles
days.objective: Use le/la/un/une correctly and learn nouns with their gender.
days.paretoFocus: Every French noun has a gender shown by its article. Learn each noun WITH le/la — it's the single biggest habit for accuracy.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'articles/gender' activity.
Learn 10 nouns WITH their article in the Words tab.
Label 5 objects at home with le/la.

items: Recall: which article for a feminine noun?
Say 3 nouns with le and 3 with la.

activities.title: Les articles
activities.intro: Definite (le/la/les) = specific; indefinite (un/une/des) = a/some. Before a vowel, le/la → l'.

questions.explanation: maison is feminine → la maison / une maison.
Before a vowel, le/la elide to l'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It's a house.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a combien de chambres ?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il y a deux chambres et une cuisine.
lines.en: There are two bedrooms and a kitchen.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La salle de bain est là.

days.title: Être et avoir
days.objective: Conjugate the two most important verbs in the present.
days.paretoFocus: être (to be) and avoir (to have) describe states, possession and age, and later build the past tense. Memorise them cold.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'être/avoir' activity.
Recite both verbs aloud, all persons.
Write 4 sentences: 2 with être, 2 with avoir.

activities.title: Être et avoir
activities.intro: Irregular but essential. Say the full conjugations aloud.

items.hr: je suis / tu es / il est
items.en: I am / you are / he is

items.hr: nous sommes / vous êtes / ils sont
items.en: we are / you are / they are
I have / you have / he has

items.hr: nous avons / vous avez / ils ont
items.en: we have / you have / they have
I am hungry. (lit. I have hunger)

questions.explanation: avoir faim = to be hungry (have hunger).

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'They are tired.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On a de l'eau à la maison.

days.title: Le présent : -ER, aller, faire
days.objective: Conjugate regular -ER verbs and the key irregulars aller and faire.
days.paretoFocus: Regular -ER verbs are ~90% of all French verbs and share ONE ending pattern. Add aller and faire and you can talk about daily life.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'present tense' activity.
Conjugate parler, aller, faire aloud.
Write 5 sentences about your day.

items: Conjugate a regular -ER verb from memory.

activities.title: Le présent
activities.intro: Drop -er, add -e/-es/-e/-ons/-ez/-ent. aller and faire are irregular.

items.hr: je parle / tu parles / il parle
nous parlons / vous parlez / ils parlent
je vais / tu vas / il va / nous allons / vous allez / ils vont
je fais / tu fais / il fait / nous faisons / vous faites / ils font

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I'm going to work.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu fais le week-end ?
lines.en: What do you do on weekends?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais au marché et je cuisine.
lines.en: I go to the market and I cook.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Shall we play together on Sunday?

days.title: La famille
days.objective: Talk about your family with possessive adjectives.
days.paretoFocus: Family vocabulary plus mon/ma/mes lets you describe the people in your life — a very common DELF topic.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'family' activity.
Learn family words in the Words tab.
Describe your family aloud in 5 sentences.

activities.title: La famille
activities.intro: Possessives agree with the THING owned: mon/ma/mes.

items.hr: mon père, ma mère, mes parents
items.en: my father, my mother, my parents

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have two sisters.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as des frères et sœurs ?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai un frère et une sœur.
lines.en: Yes, I have a brother and a sister.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: My parents live in the United States.

days.title: Manger et boire
days.objective: Order food and drink and use the partitive du/de la.
days.paretoFocus: The partitive (du/de la/des = 'some') is essential at any café or table — the most practical A1 grammar point.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'food/restaurant' activity.
Learn food words in the Words tab.
Role-play ordering a meal aloud.

items: Recall du / de la / de l' / des.

activities.title: Au restaurant
activities.intro: 'some' of an uncountable = du (m.) / de la (f.) / de l' (vowel) / des (pl.).

questions.explanation: eau starts with a vowel → de l'.
viande is feminine → de la viande.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The bill, please.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je voudrais une salade et de l'eau.
lines.en: Yes, I'd like a salad and some water.

days.title: La négation et les questions
days.objective: Make negatives with ne…pas and ask questions three ways.
days.paretoFocus: ne…pas and the three question forms let you say what ISN'T and ask about anything — the backbone of conversation.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'negation/questions' activity.
Turn 5 sentences negative aloud.
Ask 5 questions three different ways.

items: Make a sentence negative with ne…pas.
Ask the same question three ways.

activities.title: Ne… pas et les questions
activities.intro: Negation wraps the verb: ne + verb + pas. Three ways to ask: intonation, est-ce que, inversion.

items.en: I'm not hungry. (ne → n' before a vowel)

items.hr: Tu viens ? / Est-ce que tu viens ? / Viens-tu ?

questions.prompt: The most formal way to ask a question is:
Make it negative: Je ___ comprends ___. (type: ne)
Build the French for: 'Where do you live?'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un peu, mais je ne comprends pas tout.
lines.en: A little, but I don't understand everything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Ah! I'm from the United States.

days.title: Le futur proche
days.objective: Talk about plans with aller + infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: aller + infinitive is the everyday future ('going to do'). One easy pattern covers all your plans.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'futur proche' activity.
Say 5 plans for this week aloud.
Make 3 of them negative.

items: Form the near future with aller.

activities.title: Le futur proche
activities.intro: Present of aller + an infinitive = near future.

items.hr: Je ne vais pas travailler demain.
items.en: I'm not going to work tomorrow.
What are you going to do?

questions.prompt: Complete: Demain, je ___ visiter le musée. (aller)
Build the French for: 'Are you leaving tomorrow?'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu vas faire ce soir ?
lines.en: What are you going to do tonight?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: I'm going to watch a film.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Tomorrow I'm going to visit Paris.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super ! Tu vas prendre le train ?
lines.en: Great! Are you going to take the train?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je vais partir tôt le matin.
lines.en: Yes, I'm going to leave early in the morning.

days.title: Le passé composé (introduction)
days.objective: Talk about the past with avoir + past participle.
days.paretoFocus: The passé composé reports completed past actions. Most verbs use avoir + a past participle — the single most useful past tense.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'passé composé' activity.
Say 5 things you did yesterday aloud.
Make 2 of them negative.

items: Form the passé composé with avoir.

items.hr: J'ai fait / j'ai vu / j'ai pris
items.en: I did / I saw / I took (irregular participles)

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We saw a film.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait ce week-end ?
lines.en: What did you do this weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai visité un musée et j'ai mangé au restaurant.
lines.en: I visited a museum and ate at a restaurant.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais je n'ai pas dormi beaucoup.
lines.en: Yes, but I didn't sleep much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu le nouveau film ?
lines.en: Did you see the new film?
